titlestmt.titleproper: Clarke and Van Ness family papers2166
titlestmt.author: Compiled by K. Johnson

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-02-13 18:12:02 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Clarke and Van Ness family papers
did.physdesc: Papers, correspondence, photograph albums, publications, clippings, reports, inventories.
did.abstract: Includes official papers and correspondence concerning the Civil War service of Mr. Clarke's father, James E. Clarke, and his maternal grandfather, Zolman Van Ness, as well as correspondence, campaign publications, and clippings concerning the fund-raising drive of the National Contributors Committee.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes official papers and correspondence concerning the Civil War service of Mr. Clarke's father, James E. Clarke, and his maternal grandfather, Zolman Van Ness; autograph and photograph albums of his mother, Julia Marie Van Ness Clarke, and maternal grandmother, Gloriana Gidney Van Ness; letters, veterans' pension documents, estate inventories, other business and legal papers, genealogy, sheet music, and miscellaneous items of the Clarke, Fowler, Gidney, Van Ness, and Van Zile families; Albany, Dutchess, Rensselaer and Saratoga Counties. Also a scrapbook of correspondence, campaign publications, and clippings concerning the fund-raising drive of the National Contributors Committee, of which Mr. Clarke was executive secretary, conducted on behalf of the Republican National Committee during the 1924 presidential election campaign. Also, a report (Dec. 1924) of Payson & Clarke Company, New York City, concerning a proposed drive to sell American Realty Corporation stock as a means of financing a new American Woman's Association club house in midtown Manhattan; selected reports, plans, and form letters (1 v., Jan. to June, 1925); and an album of photographs of persons and events associated with this undertaking. Also includes Civil War memorabilia of James E. Clarke.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Clarke and Van Ness family papers, #2166. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Women -- New York (State) -- Societies and clubs.
Inventories of decedents\' estates -- New York (State).
Families -- New York (State) -- Saratoga County.
Families -- New York (State) -- Rensselaer County.
Families -- New York (State) -- Dutchess County.
Families -- New York (State) -- Albany County.
Military pensions -- United States -- Civil War, 1861-1865.
United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865 -- Veterans.
controlaccess.corpname: Republican National Committee. National Contributors Committee

did.unittitle: Bible: (large, red and gold, leather-bound, clasp) - family Bible of James Irving Clarke, son of James E. Clarke. Contains records of births, marriages, and deaths; came through maternal line: Gidney, Fowler, Van Ness, Clarke. Punch paper bookmark, "God is Love."

Bible: (pocket size, black, leather-bound) - note by J. and C. "Presented by my mother, Julia M[arie] Van Ness, to my father before they were married and my father carried this pocket bible throughout the Civil War"
Album: (small, brown, leather-bound, raised design, clasp) - belonged to Julia Van Ness; contains unlabelled photographs
Autograph Album: ("Rosebud Album," black) - autographs of friends 1866-1868 while Julia Van Ness Clarke was a student at Hudson Vale Institute, contains a photograph of her. Lansingburgh, Albany Co.

Album (55 items): (small, dark, green, "Photographs") - only three are identified by Mrs. Clarke. Some appear to have been taken of Eskimos and some in Japan. 3, Japanese girls; 4, Eskimos; 9, Indians (Mrs. J.I.C. - remnants of tribe living in or near Saratoga); also, Saratoga and vicinity scenes

Velvet folder: containing loose photographs of U.S. Grant, Lincoln, Booth, Lincoln's Cabinet; some of family pictures identified, souvenir album of Pasadena, 19-women, 25-men, 10-group or misc. Taken at Troy, Ballston, Saratoga, Newburgh, Poughkeepsie, N.Y.C., Atlantic City. Mr. and Mrs. JEC, JIC, Mrs. Sarah Stillman Locke
Autograph Album: (black, leather-bound, raised design) - belonged to Gloriana Gidney Van Ness, grandmother of James Irving Clarke,
Album: (large, brown, raised design, leather-bound, "Julia") - photographs of some family members and fiends identified, Lincoln, Sherman, Beecher, and H.B. Stowe, Horance Greeley, John Brown

Memoranda notebook: (black cardboard cover) - belonged to Gloriana G. Van Ness, dated Jan. 1, 1858. Only five pages filled with notes of interest on the size of various buildings and scriptural verses on love
Music book: (black hard cover) - "Mendelssohn's Songs without Words,"

Three volumes of bound sheet music: (large, black, hard cover) - on cover, "Julia M. Clarke." On binding, "Music Vol. I." Vol. 2 and Vol. 3 respectively. Piano solos, duets, transcriptions and songs, "Battle of Manassas," by Blind Tom. Most of these pieces must be typical of the period because few if any have survived as a part of the piano repertoire

Photograph album: "American Woman's Association Campaign, Jan. 1, 1925-June 15, 1925. A Selection of Photographs Property of Payson and Clarke (J.I. Clarke) Co. 385 Madison Ave., New York." Pictures of Marie Dressler, Carol McComas, Billie Burke, Gloria Swanson, etc.
Bound volume: "American Woman's Association Campaign, Jan. 1, 1925-June 25, 1925. A Selection of Reports, Plans, Form Letters, etc." For a fund-raising campaign to build a clubhouse
Album: "Literature, Press Clippings, and Letters Regarding the Campaign for Funds Conducted by the National Contributors Committee for the Republican National Committee in the 1924 Campaign"
Marriage certificate of James E. Clarke and Julia M. Van Ness, Apr. 25, 1870, Troy, New York
Citizenship paper of James E. Clarke, from England, Oct. 16, 1866. Rensselaer Co. Ct.
Envelope containing 50-cent Confederate bill

Photostat of letter dated Oct. 4, 1864, from Executive Mansion and signed Abraham Lincoln concerning the pardon of Roswell McIntyre. Note at bottom, "Taken from the body of R. McIntyre at the battle of Five Forks, VA, 1865"
Folder containing Preliminary Report to the American Woman's Association by Payson and Clarke Co.
Typescript, "Siege and Capture of Port Hudson," 10 pages. Presumably written by JEC. Action took place at Port Hudson, LA,

Letter from James E. Clarke to Capt. Ed. N. Ketchum, Jan. 27, 1914, eight pages (typed cb.) relating Civil War adventures. JEC tells ENK what happened during rest of battle after ENK was wounded and taken to the rear. 19th Corps, General [cuvier] Grover's division. Between Newton and Middletown, VA (probably Battle of Cedar Creek, Oct. 19, 1864. Reference to Sheraton's ride

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JEC was living in Saratoga Springs when above letter was written. Ketchum was in Galveston, Texas. The two had served together in the 128th Reg't., N.Y.S. Vols.)

did.unittitle: Typescript carbon, "How the Slave Mother Found Her Son," by James E. Clarke, 5 pages
Envelope: "Zolman Van Ness Papers" (Zalmon?)
Marriage certificate of Zolman Van Ness and Gloriana Gidney, Feb. 4, 1847. Milton (Saratoga Co.?)
Longhand Certificate stating that Zolman Van Ness volunteered Apr. 20, 1861. Camp Willard
Longhand Pass May 21, 1863 for Van Ness to visit Hq. and return to hospital
Longhand Discharge June 2, 1863 from hospital for Van Ness
Certificate of Discharge from Service May 26, 1863 for Zolman Van Ness
Manuscript Copy of "The Inscription on the Momument at Lindiwold (Lindenwald?) at Kirkhook erected to the memory of Peter Van Ness, copied by James E. Clarke,
Envelope addressed to Z. Van Ness from Dept. of Interior
Certificate of Elgibility for Pension for Z. Van Ness,
Envelope: "Other Papers Old"
Warrenty Deed between Otis G. Clarke and wife Amelia S., Jesse and Mary Van Zile, and wife to Sarah M. McClure, Feb. 25, 1864. Land, Troy, Rensselaer Co.
Citation to appear at Surrogate's Court for the Probate of the will of Solomon W. Gidney, Apr. 17, 1865. Dutchess Co. Addressed to Sarah Maria Gidney, Ballston, Saratoga Co.
"James E. Clarke with Ensign S. Lockrow Contract to Convey, dated Oct. 8, 1908. Land and house, furniture and fixtures for sum of $7,100, Saratoga Springs, N.Y.
Envelope: "J.E. Clarke Papers"
Statement of James E. Clarke as to Disablement to War Claims and Pensions, 1893 (?)
Pension Certificate of James E. Clarke,
Order for Transportation on the B&O for James E. Clarke from Harpers Ferry, VA to Baltimore, MD
Affidavit of Isaac J. Mickle on behalf of War Claim of James E. Clarke
Letters of James E. Clarke to Gen. B. Raum, Commissioner of Pensions,
Deed, Isaac J. Flansburgh and wife to James E. Clarke, Saratoga Springs, N.Y.,
Receipt for payment of $2.00 for Saddle horse used by J.F.C. in G.A.R. parade, pd.,
Check for $10.00 to J. Irving Clarke for Julia M. Clarke,
Promisory note in the amount of $34 from Gloriana Van Ness to James E. Clarke,
Affidavit of J.E. Clarke on behalf of War Claim of Isaac J. Mickle,
Rent Receipt, Jan. 18, 1887 - Gloriana Van Ness received payment from James E. Clarke
Receipt of Dr. Kingsley from James E. Clarke, Oct. 27, 1873, for treating cancer of breast for wife
Envelope containing letter from Isaac Van Dyck to James E. Clarke, Sept. 25, 1888, re disability claim of JEC
Envelope addressed to J.E. Clarke conatining letter from E.M. Hinman, June 19, 1888, re disability
Envelope addressed to James E. Clarke containing letter from James E. Clarke to B.F. Page, Jan. 23, 1893, and on the bottom the reply of Benj. F. Page, re disability
Discharge from Service for James I. Clarke, given at Savannah, Georgia,
Certificate of Inclosure of Certificate of Pension, Nov. 26, 1889 (JEC?)
Letter to J. E. Clarke from (?) Van Ness,
Envelope
Saratoga Surrogate's Court Inventory of the Estate of Zolman Van Ness
Saratoga Surrogate's Court Inventory of the Estate of Gloriana Van Ness,
Civil War Objects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes an object that is noted as being from an Egyptian mummy.

did.unittitle: American anti-slavery medal
Torpedo wire
Part of a torpedo rescued from a river
Fuse to a 15" shell from the Monitor
Unidentified piece of metal
Fuse to a 15" shell
Fuse to a 17" shell-gunboat guns
Grape Shot for Cannon
Unidentified piece of metal, possibly a fuse to a large shell
Unidentified piece of metal, flat oval
Section of a chain
Unidentified pieces of metal, resemble gears
Connection to 2 torpedo wires
Unidentified piece of metal
Unidentified pieces of gun
Canteen carried through Civil War by JEC
Lead ball

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note by James E. Clarke: "This bullet was shot at our skirmish line while fighting in front of the rebel brest works at Port Hudson, May 27, 1863, and came within a fraction of an inch of finding a lodgement in my head (good luck). James E. Clarke, Sergt. Co. E. 128th N.Y.S. Vols."

did.unittitle: Briar pipe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JEC: "This Pipe was used in the War of the Slave holders rebellion by James E. Clarke, Sergt. Co. E. 128th regt. N.Y.S. Vols."

did.unittitle: Leather pocketbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JEC: "This pocket book was carried through the War of the Slave holders rebellion by James E. Clarke. Sergeant Company E. 128th Regiment N.Y.S. Volunteers (contains no writing)

did.unittitle: Cartridges

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JEC: "Cartridges brought home from the War of the Slave holders rebellion by Sergt. James E. Clarke, 128th Regt. N.Y.S. Vols."

did.unittitle: Mummy Cloth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note reads: "Taken from an Egyptian mummy over 3300 years old. This was a piece of the cloth wrapped around the body. It was a lady."

did.unittitle: Hand-carved bone ring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: , JEC: "This bone ring was made by me while in camp at Chalmette, LA., about Jan. 1863. James E. Clarke"

did.unittitle: Human hair watch fob
Watch chain with gold lover's knot as a fob
Bullet shot at Port Hudson, with note describing the event
